parameters.text:
Hi! I'm NerdHug — your nerdy study buddy!
I give warm hugs and cold, hard facts.
Here's what we can do together:

/learn [URL] - Send me any article link and I'll read it for you, pull out the key points, and make a neat summary.

/quiz - When you're ready, I'll quiz you on what you've learned. 
5 questions, instant feedback, no judgment!

Ready? Send me /learn followed by a link and let's get smart together!
⏳ Fetching and analyzing the content... Please wait.

=You are a TEACHER AI agent. Analyze the following learning material and create a structured summary.

URL: {{ $('Clean HTML').item.json.url }}
Raw title: {{ $('Clean HTML').item.json.rawTitle }}

CONTENT:
{{ $('Clean HTML').item.json.content }}

---

Please respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no backticks, no extra text) in this exact format:
{
  "title": "A clear, concise title for this material",
  "difficulty": "beginner" or "intermediate" or "advanced",
  "mainConcepts": ["concept1", "concept2", "concept3"],
  "keyPoints": [
    "Key point 1 — a clear and informative statement",
    "Key point 2 — a clear and informative statement",
    "Key point 3 — a clear and informative statement",
    "Key point 4 — a clear and informative statement",
    "Key point 5 — a clear and informative statement"
  ],
  "summary": "A 2-3 sentence overview of the material"
}

IMPORTANT:
- Provide exactly 5-7 key points
- Key points must be specific to THIS content, not generic
- Difficulty should reflect the actual complexity of the material
- Response must be valid JSON only, no other text

IMPORTANT: Always respond in the SAME LANGUAGE as the original content. If the article is in Russian, write the summary in Russian. If in English, write in English.

options.systemMessage: You are a Teacher AI. You analyze learning materials and create structured, educational summaries. Always respond with valid JSON only.
You are an Examiner AI. You create educational quizzes to test comprehension. Always respond with valid JSON only.

value.quizSessionId: Execute previous nodes to use input data
